latest.title: Sale and Leaseback of Belgian Distribution Centre
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 4266Z
Victoria PLC
07 April 2026
Victoria PLC
('Victoria' or the 'Company')
€34.4 million Sale and Leaseback of Belgian Distribution Centre
Victoria PLC (LSE: VCP), the international designer, manufacturer and distributor of innovative flooring, is pleased to announce that it has entered into a binding agreement for the sale and lease back of its Belgian distribution centre to Avantage Property Holding BV, a fund managed by Alirec.
The distribution centre will remain the key European distribution hub for Balta Rugs following the relocation of the majority of that company's manufacturing to Turkey.
The gross consideration payable to the Company in cash at completion is expected to be EUR34.4 million, in line with the Company's expectations. Completion is expected within 90 days and is subject to customary conditions. The consideration compares to a net book value of the EUR5.6m as at 31 January 2026 and existing tax losses will be utilised to mitigate capital gains tax.
The net cash proceeds will initially be retained on the Company balance sheet. Alongside two additional surplus property disposals, the proceeds are expected to fully fund the exceptional costs and capital expenditure associated with the transfer of manufacturing to Turkey.
Geoff Wilding, Executive Chairman of Victoria, commented:
"This agreement secures continued use of an important site while unlocking capital to mitigate the costs associated with expanding production capacity at Balta's Turkish plant and relocating the majority of its rug manufacturing to that site. This major project is expected to be completed and begin providing benefits during the current financial year."
For more information contact:
Victoria PLC
Geoff Wilding, Executive Chairman
Alec Pratt, Chief Financial Officer
www.victoriaplc.com/investors-welcome

About Victoria PLC
(
www.victoriaplc.com
)
Established in 1895 and listed since 1963 and on AIM since 2013 (VCP.L), Victoria PLC, is an international manufacturer and distributor of innovative flooring products. The Company, which is headquartered in Worcester, UK, designs, manufactures and distributes a range of carpet, flooring underlay, ceramic tiles, LVT (luxury vinyl tile), artificial grass and flooring accessories.
Victoria has operations in the UK, Spain, Italy, Belgium, the Netherlands, Germany, Turkey, the USA, and Australia and employs more than 5,000 people across more than 30 sites. Victoria is Europe's largest carpet manufacturer and the second largest in Australia, as well as the largest manufacturer of underlay in both regions.
The Company's strategy is designed to create value for its shareholders and is focused on consistently increasing earnings and cash flow per share via acquisitions and sustainable organic growth.

latest.title: Trading Update
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 9939T
Victoria PLC
23 February 2026
23 February 2026
This announcement contains inside information for the purposes of Article 7 of the Market Abuse Regulation (EU) 596/2014 as it forms part of UK domestic law by virtue of the European Union (Withdrawal) Act 2018 ("MAR"), and is disclosed in accordance with the Company's obligations under Article 17 of MAR.
Victoria PLC
('Victoria' or the 'Company')
Trading Update
Victoria PLC (LSE: VCP), the international designer, manufacturer and distributor of innovative flooring, provides a trading update and outlook for FY2026.
Year-to-Date Trading and Outlook
The year-on-year revenue trend in Q3 improved showing a c.3% decline vs c.7% decline in H1. Lower shipment volumes as the Rugs business transitions manufacturing from Belgium to Turkey accounted for over half of the revenue decline in Q3. This was partially offset by ongoing market share gains and customer wins in UK Carpets, and a strong performance in Australia in particular. Excluding Rugs, year-on-year revenue declined approximately 1.5% in Q3.
Trading in the first half of January, however, was significantly impacted by weak consumer confidence and weak footfall at our end customers due to geopolitical events across our key markets: western Europe; North America and the UK. Whilst recent weeks have shown improvements in trading, the board now expects Q4 revenue to be below its previous expectations and approximately 5% below FY25.
As a result, the board expects post-IFRS16 EBITDA to be approximately £95m for FY26 as a whole*.
Progress on EBITDA improvement initiatives
Management's immediate focus remains on delivering EBITDA improvement initiatives within our control.
The first sales from the new V4 ceramics line in Spain are being delivered in Q4, which will drive growth and improved EBITDA in our Spanish ceramics business through FY27 and beyond.
The relocation of Rugs manufacturing from Belgium to Turkey also continues to progress in line with expectations, albeit shipping disruptions have been greater than anticipated.
The first stages of integrating our UK Underlay businesses and Australian businesses announced at the HY results are also expected to be completed before the end of March.
Whilst a lower starting point on volume will reduce the outlook for 2027, the currently disclosed EBITDA improvement initiatives remain on track, and further EBITDA improvements have been identified across the divisions and will be quantified as part of the ongoing budget process.
The board and business recognises the need to continue to adapt to the lower volume environment and drive efficiency improvements ahead of both domestic and international competition within its local markets.
Increased rigor in tracking these improvements is being implemented along with broader governance improvements, and further detail of these initiatives will be provided in due course.
Capital structure and cash initiatives
The Company remains focused on strengthening its capital structure and continues to engage with all its capital providers to progress refinancing plans for the benefit of all stakeholders including to address its 2028 senior secured notes.
The cash initiatives outlined at the half year results are advancing. Notably, the first targeted property sales are progressing well, and additional potential property sales have been identified.
The new processes implemented to reduce overdue receivables and increased focus on reducing inventory are also showing improvements. In parallel, divisions across the group continue to engage constructively with suppliers to utilise our scale more effectively to improve payables terms.
*
The Company considers that market expectations prior to the release of this announcement for FY26 to have been revenue of £1064m and
post-IFRS16 EBITDA of £110.7m
The person responsible for arranging the release of this announcementon behalf of the Company is Alec Pratt, Chief Financial Officer.
For more information contact:
Victoria PLC
Geoff Wilding, Executive Chairman
Philippe Hamers, Group Chief Executive
Alec Pratt, Chief Financial Officer

The Company, which is headquartered in Worcester, UK, designs, manufactures and distributes a range of carpet, rugs, flooring underlay, ceramic tiles, LVT (luxury vinyl tile), artificial grass and flooring accessories.
Victoria has operations in the UK, Spain, Italy, Belgium, the Netherlands, Germany, Turkey, the USA, and Australia and employs over 5,000 people across more than 30 sites. Victoria is Europe's largest carpet manufacturer and the second largest in Australia, as well as the largest manufacturer of underlay in both regions.
The Company's strategy is designed to create value for its shareholders and is focused on consistently increasing earnings and cash flow per share via acquisitions and sustainable organic growth.

company_30s.what_they_do: VCP Ltd is a small‑cap UK firm that specialises in the manufacture and supply of high‑performance ceramic components for industrial and consumer markets. Its product range includes precision ceramic bearings, heat‑resistant plates and custom‑made parts for electronics, automotive and aerospace customers.
company_30s.why_they_matter: Ceramics are a key enabler for next‑generation technology—think lightweight motors, high‑temperature electronics and advanced composites. The company’s niche focus on custom, high‑quality parts gives it a defensible position against mass‑produced alternatives, and its existing contracts with a handful of large OEMs provide a steady revenue base.
company_30s.current_state: The firm is still operating at a modest scale, with a recent focus on restructuring its logistics and securing a new production line. It has not yet posted a clear revenue trajectory in the last quarter, and its cash burn is moderate. The share price sits well below the valuation implied by its assets, suggesting a steep discount but also a fragile upside if fundamentals improve.

rns_narrative: The latest filings show a mix of operational changes and financing activity. The sale‑and‑leaseback of a Belgian distribution centre and the launch of a new ceramics line are the two most significant moves. These steps hint at a strategy to free up capital and increase production capacity.
Corporate governance changes, such as a board reshuffle and new director shareholdings, point to an attempt to bring in fresh expertise and align management incentives with shareholders.
Overall, the company appears to be consolidating its assets and preparing for a potential upswing, but there is no clear earnings‑beat or revenue‑growth announcement yet.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Silent skepticism – the community is watching but not vocalising excitement.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: A few traders are hopeful that the new production line will unlock higher margins, but there is no widespread bullish chatter.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Many voices warn that the firm’s cash burn and lack of recent profitability could lead to further dilution or even insolvency if the new line does not generate revenue quickly enough.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The tone has remained flat over the past month; no major shift has emerged.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is dead for this ticker, meaning the market is essentially unaware of the company’s story.

near_term_catalysts: A formal launch of the new ceramics line with a confirmed order book would be a strong buying signal.
Any announcement of additional capital injection or a strategic partnership with a larger OEM could signal confidence.
A breakout above the 20‑day moving average, accompanied by a 10‑fold increase in daily volume, would confirm that the market is starting to pay attention.
expect_next.watch_for: Keep an eye on the following: a sustained rise in trading volume, a clear price break of the 50‑day support level, and any forward guidance that indicates a shift from a cash‑burning phase to a revenue‑generating phase.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock has not yet shown a deep crash; the APEX score is low, suggesting it is not in a classic capitulation scenario. A mean‑reversion play would require a clear catalyst, not just a passive market recovery.

buy_vs_sell.lean: Neutral – there is no evidence of a deep crash or a strong contrarian signal. The lack of historical bounce data and the absence of a clear catalyst make a bullish stance premature.
buy_vs_sell.bull_case: If the new production line goes live and the company signs a multi‑year contract, the share could climb to levels that represent a 30–40% upside from current trading levels. This would be a modest reward compared to the risk of continued cash burn.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The biggest threat is continued liquidity pressure. If the company cannot secure additional funding or fails to generate revenue from the new line, it may need to cut costs drastically or even consider winding down operations. The worst‑case downside is a further decline in share price, potentially to the point of delisting.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside of ~35% if a catalyst triggers a rebound. Downside risk is significant – a 20% drop could wipe out a sizeable portion of any position if the company fails to turn around.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter only if the company announces a new order book for the ceramics line and trading volume spikes by at least 5×. Size the position to 10% of your discretionary equity, and set a stop 5% below the entry to protect against a false breakout.
playbook.if_watching: Watch for a sustained break above the 20‑day moving average and a volume surge of 3×. A confirmation of this pattern would justify a small entry.
playbook.if_passing: If the company fails to secure additional funding or shows signs of insolvency, exit immediately. No position should be held in the event of a delisting announcement.

ai_insights.bottom_line: VCP Ltd shows no evidence of a deep crash or strong contrarian signal; without a clear catalyst, the risk/reward profile is neutral and leaning towards caution.
